body_txt: A character has suffered an amusing injury , usually of the blunt force to the head variety. Dazed, he will say something really random and Non Sequitur , normally just before going out like a light. It seems that with proper application of blunt trauma, the human skull becomes a radio receiver tuned to a random station somewhere in the world. Three common phrases used are " How many fingers am I holding up? " (helpful bystander), some variation of " I don't wanna go to school today, mommy " (victim) and "Anybody get the number of that truck?" (ditto). The character may also mistake someone closest for someone else during such. A subtrope of Non Sequitur and Parting-from-Consciousness Words , which also includes the cases where the parting words actually make sense. Overlaps with Intoxication Ensues when caused by drugs or alcohol. May result in Delirious Misidentification . See also Concussions Get You High . In less serious works, tends to come hand-in-hand with Circling Birdies . Contrast with Post-Op Ditziness , where the silly lines come after a patient wakes up from anesthesia— BONK! How dare that jaguar steal my pantyhose?!? The corresponding situation where a character says something random while regaining consciousness, is Waking Non Sequitur . If a character says something random while unconscious, that's Talking in Your Sleep . Spoonerism is a common varia— BONK! And THAT, my good senator, is why my brother likes to live on the wild rice, and won't eat his track and beans! See Tranquilized Mid-Sentence for when they say something nonsensical due to a tranq da— BONK! So long, Ladies and Germs ! Remember to flip your turtles! Compare and contrast Mid-Sentence Mind Warp , in which the bizarre statement emerges much slower, not necessarily due to head trauma.
